content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 27, 1990


Donald M. Zimring
Assistant Superintendent
Las Virgenes Unified School District
30961 West Agoura Road, Suite 100
Westlake Village, CA 91361

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-90-167

Dear Mr. Zimring:

We have received your request for advice under the conflict
of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

The Las Virgenes Unified School District (the District) is
principally located in Los Angeles County. Five years ago, the
District annexed a small portion of Ventura County. The District
presently has its conflict of interest code filing requirements
with Los Angeles County. You are now asking if the District can
continue its reporting practice to Los Angeles County.

CONCLUSION

Since the District now has jurisdiction in more than one
county, the Fair Political Practices Commission is the appropriate
code reviewing body, and as such, must approve the District's
conflict of interest code and provide for the place of filing for
the statements of economic interest.


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Page Two
March 27, 1990


DISCUSSION

The Las Virgenes Unified School District has been principally
located in Los Angeles County. About five years ago a small
portion of area in neighboring Ventura County was annexed with the
District to accommodate students residing in the Bell Canyon area.
The Bell Canyon area was originally part of the Simi Unified
School District, but a mountain range physically divided Bell
Canyon students from the Simi Unified School District. Residents
in the Bell Canyon area successfully petitioned for the annexation
to the Las Virgenes Unified School District to alleviate the long
and difficult commute for students to attend school.

The District's employees have been filing their statements of
economic interests under a code developed by the County of Los
Angeles. The District seeks to continue the existing practice of
reporting to the County rather than to the Fair Political
Practices Commission.


ANALYSIS

The Political Reform Act requires every agency to adopt and
promulgate a conflict of interest code. A code designates the
positions within the agency who are in decisionmaking capacities
and further sets out what types of financial interests must be
disclosed by employees in those designated positions. (Section
87300-87302.) All conflict of interest codes, and any amendments
to it, must be approved by the appropriate code reviewing body.
(Section 87303.) The code reviewing body for any agency with
jurisdiction in more than one county is the Fair Political
Practices Commission. (Section 82011.)

There is nothing in the Political Reform Act, or its
implementing regulations, which excepts from the definition of a
multi-county agency an agency with boundaries that cross county
lines if only a very small portion of the jurisdiction, or
property held, is in the neighboring or second county. The
Commission has no statutory authority to create this type of
exception. Accordingly, the Las Virgenes Unified School District
is a local government agency with jurisdiction in more than one
county and the appropriate code reviewing body is the Fair
Political Practices Commission.

The District will need to amend to its existing conflict of
interest code. We have given the code a cursory review but will
identify specific problems and concerns which need to be corrected
under a separate letter.

Page Three
March 27, 1990


Since the code provides that annual statements must be filed
by February of each year, this change will not have any effect on
your annual statements for this year.  Should any designated
employees assume or leave office during this transition period,
please retain their completed statements of economic interests
with your District.

     If you have any questions, or wish to discuss this, please
feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        By:  Jeanette E. Turvill
                                             Political Reform Consultant
                                             Legal Division

KED/JET/jt
